name: Kolachkes
recipeIngredient: Confectioners' sugar for rolling and sprinkling
Jam, jelly or preserves of choice
recipeInstructions:
Preparation time: 2 1/2 hours
Chilling time: Overnight
Cooking time: 12 to 15 minutes
1. Beat butter and cream cheese in a large bowl with an electric mixer until light.
2. Beat in flour and whipping cream until well mixed. Divide dough into four portions.
3. Wrap each portion in plastic wrap and refrigerate overnight.
4. Preheat oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit. Have ungreased baking sheets ready.
5. Sprinkle the work surface and rolling pin generously with confectioners' sugar.
6. Roll out one dough portion at a time to about 1/4-inch thickness using a small (2-inch diameter) round cutter or glass to cut out cookies.
7. Transfer cookies to ungreased baking sheets, leaving 1 or 2 inches between each cookie.
8. Make a small indentation in the center of each cookie with your fingertip.
9. Fill indentations scantily with jam, jelly, preserves, cream cheese, or nut filling (if using).
10. Bake until bottoms are lightly browned, about 12 to 15 minutes.
11. Remove from oven and let cool on wire racks.
12. Sprinkle confectioners' sugar generously over cookies while still warm.
Cream cheese filling:
Beat together 1 package (8 ounces) softened cream cheese, 1 egg yolk, 1/2 cup confectioners' sugar, and 1 teaspoon pure vanilla extract until well mixed.
Nut filling:
Cook 1 cup coarsely ground walnuts in 2 tablespoons butter with 1/3 cup granulated sugar and 1 teaspoon pure vanilla extract until nuts turn golden. Let cool.
